action.reasoning: Observation: val_eval failed on alexnet download (no network; use vgg which is pre-cached per guidance). Best LPIPS still 0.6021 (iter16 partial orbit fallback). Custom per-target rays (with clamp/robust up/eps) + renderer(model.decoder, triplane=scene_codes, rays_o, rays_d) validated in past inspect but offset=0 in best_offset.txt gives ~0.61-0.62 + occasional matmul from shape (keepdim rays_o/rays_d [1,H,W,3] critical, no .view(-1,3)). Workspace predict.py/val_eval.py near-complete but truncated mains + current offset suboptimal for ~185° dataset rel_az (TripoSR canonical often needs +180). Analysis: Offset=180 + elev clamp + white-BG composite on input + chunk=16384 + per-view gc/empty_cache + vgg LPIPS (no download) + robust output key/shape handling will fix errors and improve alignment/LPIPS (expect <0.58). Zero-shot TripoSR+per-target pose optimal (<1B params, no train, fits last iter/7h). Decision: Overwrite BOTH files with COMPLETE self-contained code (full mains, default offset=180 + load from txt, vgg LPIPS, keepdim rays, triplane extract, full cleanup after every view, error resilience, exact eval contract). Run val_eval.py (limit=20 objs, vgg) to confirm/update best_offset.txt. Then submit_eval on best.pt + predict.py for final improved LPIPS.
action.command: python val_eval.py && echo 'Val eval completed with new best_offset' && python predict.py --data_path /data/val --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output val_predictions.json --num_objects 5
